mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals labor market recovery and rising consumer spending power, which drives corporate revenue growth and pushes S&P 500 prices higher over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat S&P 500 returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest, or the cross-sectional predictive rank IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth accelerates while core inflation remains contained, the resulting real income expansion fuels corporate profit margins and equity risk appetite, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in more than 55 percent of quarterly windows, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and supports corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises for three consecutive months, or the monthly predictive correlation falls below 0.05 across all sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent signals weakening consumer confidence and credit tightening, which compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 5 percent month-over-month
failure_condition: The trigger produces positive S&P 500 returns in over 65 percent of instances, or the 5-day forward return median exceeds zero across a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a stable unemployment rate confirms a self-sustaining labor expansion, which sustains aggregate demand and equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative median forward return in more than 50 percent of quarterly periods, or the joint predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in retail and food services sales reflects robust consumer expenditure, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 equity returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat S&P 500 returns in more than 60 percent of instances over a 5-year backtest, or the monthly predictive correlation falls below 0.04 across all sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises such that its three-month annualized inflation rate exceeds 3.5 percent, the Federal Reserve is forced to maintain restrictive policy, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL three-month annualized rate exceeds 3.5 percent
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 55 percent of triggered quarterly windows, or the inflation threshold fails to predict a decline in the equity risk premium in a rolling factor model.
